title: Vegetable & Black Bean Chilli

overview.description: A smoky, richly spiced chilli made with black beans, vegetables, tomatoes and sweetcorn.

ingredients: 2 x 400g tins black beans, drained and rinsed
method: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan or casserole over medium heat.
Add the onion, red pepper and carrots.
Add the courgette and cook for a further 3–4 minutes.
Add the garlic and fresh chilli if using.
Add the cumin, oregano, smoked paprika, sweet paprika and ground coriander.
Stir and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the chopped tomatoes and vegetable stock.
Add the black beans and sweetcorn.
Cook uncovered for 25–35 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender and the chilli has thickened.
Mash a small portion of the black beans against the side of the pan to thicken the sauce naturally.

learning.did-you-know: Black beans are a staple ingredient in many parts of Latin America. Their creamy texture and earthy flavour make them particularly well suited to long-simmered dishes.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build a smoky, richly flavoured bean chilli and how to thicken a stew naturally by partially breaking down some of the beans.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A cheap, filling and freezer-friendly Pantry Monday meal that makes excellent use of your black beans while introducing a different flavour profile from the Indian, Levantine and North African dishes already in the collection.
chapman-tips: The chilli should be thick enough to sit on rice rather than run like a soup.
Mashing some of the beans gives body without needing flour or other thickeners.
Don't add too much chilli at the start; heat can be added at the table.
The smoked paprika adds depth but should not dominate the dish.
Lime juice at the end brightens the rich beans and tomatoes.
The chilli is often even better the next day.
For extra depth, a small amount of cocoa powder or dark chocolate can be added, although this is optional and not necessary for a family version.
notes.batch-cooking: Freezes well. Cool completely before freezing in portions.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently with a splash of water if necessary. Excellent in burritos, quesadillas or on baked potatoes.
